target.name: Thinking Machines Lab
target.founder: Mira Murati, John Schulman, Barret Zoph, Lilian Weng

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Thinking Machines Lab is a Seed-stage AI & Software company with $2B raised and a last post-money valuation of $12.0B. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company in the AI & Software sector, including Sakana AI, Anthropic, xAI, and Palantir (PLTR).

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement applied to the $12.0B base, implies a valuation of $30.0B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Liquid AI ($2.4B Series A), Field AI ($2.0B Series A), Sakana AI ($1.0B Seed), and Twelve Labs ($250M Series A)—yields a median of $1.5B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $34.5B, with a median of $15.8B. The substantial spread reflects meaningful tension between methods: peer-median anchoring to same-stage companies produces a materially lower implied value, while stage-progression extrapolation from current post-money assumes significant value accretion. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for this particular company's profile and the representativeness of the peer set, given concentration among later-stage Series A companies in the comparable universe. Resolution requires clarity on company-specific risk factors and execution trajectory.
